sevenDay.synopsis: Cigs are MVFR in the Class D, but there will be some patchy IFR cigs for the next hour or two. Then we have a VFR, hazard-free PM in store. I think the N1 remains VFR with cigs moving in after the period ends. Tomorrow starts off IFR with improvements by 09L, but cigs return between 22-23L tomorrow N1. There's a chance for showers after midnight. Thursday AM looks messy as a cold front will bring cigs, showers, and ISOLD TS for most of the period. We should clear out by mid-PM, but winds will be flirting with warning criteria (G25-30kts). Friday is looking good, as does the weekend, but another frontal boundary slips thru overnight Sunday sparking a few showers. There could be some residual cigs first thing Monday AM, and winds will be gusty for AM/PM periods.
sevenDay.title: 7-DAY OUTLOOK: 10 MAR - 16 MAR
